author: codex-agent

patch.value: Implements pm-lvej and pm-6fmt scope by seeding linked-test sandbox roots with source project/global settings and extension directories while preserving sandbox isolation.
Make linked-test sandbox execution load both project and global extension/settings context so extension-defined item types validate identically to workspace runs.
Linked commands executed via pm test and pm test-all accept extension-defined type filters that are valid in direct workspace commands for both project and global extension roots.
Epic pm-mf5z exists and parity implementation files/tests identified.
Restore extension-defined type parity in linked sandboxes
Eliminates contradictory custom-type validation between direct and linked commands
Improves correctness of governance evidence in extension-enabled repositories
Linked sandbox mirrors project/global extension behavior for type validation
User-reported pm-lvej and pm-6fmt regressions block reliable automation in custom-schema projects.
Install extension-defined custom type then run same list/search command directly and through linked pm test run.
Linked sandbox and workspace commands share type-filter acceptance for extension-defined types.
Linked sandbox rejects custom type filter with core-only allowed list.
Custom-schema teams cannot rely on linked test evidence consistency.
